name: xurrent

metadata.name: xurrent
metadata.summary: A python module to interact with the Xurrent API.

documentation:
# Xurrent Module

This module is used to interact with the Xurrent API. It provides a set of classes to interact with the API.


## Usage

```python
    from xurrent.core import XurrentApiHelper
    from xurrent.requests import Request
    from xurrent.tasks import Task

    apitoken = "********"

    baseUrl = "https://api.4me.qa/v1"
    account = "tttech-it"

    logger = setup_logger(verbose=True)

    x_api_helper = XurrentApiHelper(baseUrl, apitoken, account)

    request = Request.get_by_id(x_api_helper, 10158378)
    task = Task.get_by_id(x_api_helper, 5986404)

```

methods.name: xurrent.core.JsonSerializableDict.to_dict
documentation: Ensure nested objects are converted to dictionaries.

methods.name: xurrent.core.JsonSerializableDict.to_json
documentation: Convert the dictionary to a JSON string.

methods.name: xurrent.core.XurrentApiHelper.__append_per_page
documentation:
Append the 'per_page' parameter to the URI if not already present.
\:param uri: URI to append the parameter to
\:param per_page: Number of records per page
\:return: URI with the 'per_page' parameter appended
\>>> helper = XurrentApiHelper('<https://api.example.com>', 'api_key', 'account')
\>>> helper.\_XurrentApiHelper\_\_append_per_page('<https://api.example.com/tasks>')
'<https://api.example.com/tasks?per_page=100>'
\>>> helper.\_XurrentApiHelper\_\_append_per_page('<https://api.example.com/tasks?status=open>')
'<https://api.example.com/tasks?status=open&per_page=100>'
\>>> helper.\_XurrentApiHelper\_\_append_per_page('<https://api.example.com/tasks?status=open&per_page=50>')
'<https://api.example.com/tasks?status=open&per_page=50>'
\>>> helper.\_XurrentApiHelper\_\_append_per_page('<https://api.example.com/tasks/>')
'<https://api.example.com/tasks?per_page=100>'
\>>> helper.\_XurrentApiHelper\_\_append_per_page('<https://api.example.com/tasks?per_page=50>', 100)
'<https://api.example.com/tasks?per_page=50>'

methods.name: xurrent.core.XurrentApiHelper.api_call
documentation:
Make a call to the Xurrent API with support for rate limiting and pagination.
\:param uri: URI to call
\:param method: HTTP method to use
\:param data: Data to send with the request (optional)
\:param per_page: Number of records per page for GET requests (default: 100)
\:return: JSON response from the API or aggregated data for paginated GET

methods.name: xurrent.core.XurrentApiHelper.custom_fields_to_object
documentation:
Convert a list of custom fields to a dictionary.
\:param custom_fields: List of custom fields
\:return: Dictionary containing the custom fields

```pycon
>>> helper = XurrentApiHelper('https://api.example.com', 'api_key', 'account')
>>> helper.custom_fields_to_object([{'id': 'priority', 'value': 'high'}, {'id': 'status', 'value': 'open'}])
{'priority': 'high', 'status': 'open'}
```

methods.name: xurrent.core.XurrentApiHelper.object_to_custom_fields
documentation:
Convert a dictionary to a list of custom fields.
\:param obj: Dictionary to convert
\:return: List of custom fields

```pycon
>>> helper = XurrentApiHelper('https://api.example.com', 'api_key', 'account')
>>> helper.object_to_custom_fields({'priority': 'high', 'status': 'open'})
[{'id': 'priority', 'value': 'high'}, {'id': 'status', 'value': 'open'}]
```

methods.name: xurrent.core.XurrentApiHelper.create_filter_string
documentation:
Create a filter string from a dictionary.
\:param filter: Dictionary containing the filter parameters
\:return: String containing the filter parameters
\>>> helper = XurrentApiHelper('<https://api.example.com>', 'api_key', 'account')
\>>> helper.create_filter_string({'status': 'open', 'priority': 'high'})
'status=open&priority=high'

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.get_by_id
documentation:
Retrieve a request by its ID and return it as an instance of Request.
\:param connection_object: Instance of XurrentApiHelper
\:param id: ID of the request to retrieve
\:return: Instance of Request

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.get_request
documentation:
Retrieve a request by its ID.
\:param connection_object: Instance of XurrentApiHelper
\:param id: ID of the request to retrieve
\:return: Request data

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.add_note_to_request
documentation:
Add a note to a request by its ID.
\:param connection_object: Instance of XurrentApiHelper
\:param id: ID of the request
\:param note: Dictionary containing the note data
\:return: Response from the API call

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.add_note_to_request
documentation:
Add a note to the current request instance.
\:param note: Dictionary containing the note data
\:return: Response from the API call (the note that was added)

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.get_notes
documentation:
Retrieve all notes associated with the current request instance.
\:return: List of notes

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.get_note_by_id
documentation:
Retrieve a note by its ID associated with the current request instance.
\:param note_id: ID of the note to retrieve
\:return: Note data

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.update
documentation:
Update the current request instance with new data.
\:param data: Dictionary containing updated data
\:return: Response from the API call

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.close
documentation:
Close the current request instance.
\:return: Response from the API call

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.close_and_trash
documentation:
Close and trash the current request instance.
\:return: Response from the API call

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.trash
documentation:
Trashes the current request instance.

```
param force

Whether to force the trash operation (if force: the request will be closed and trashed)

return

Response from the API call
```

methods.name: xurrent.requests.Request.create
documentation:
Create a new request and return it as an instance of Request.
\:param connection_object: Instance of XurrentApiHelper
\:param data: Dictionary containing request data
\:return: Instance of Request
